Experience

Research
Undergraduate Research Assistant · Winship Cancer Institute, Emory University (Jan 2026 – Present)
Analyzing longitudinal survival and clinical trial data from 35 PDAC patients (77 samples) across a Phase 1b immunotherapy trial. Integrated 71 cytokine/growth factor biomarkers with clinical outcomes, profiled immune cell composition using CyTOF/mIHC, and conducted multi-omic analyses linking CHIP mutations to immune suppression phenotypes. Co-first author paper in progress.
Undergraduate Research Assistant · Emory University School of Medicine (Sep 2024 – Present)
Translational research in Dr. Fridovich-Keil’s lab on classic galactosemia. Modeled longitudinal and cross-sectional clinical data from 500+ patients in R using LMMs and MMRMs. Published in Journal of Inherited Metabolic Disease (2026).
Research Trainee (SIBDS) · Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health (May – Jul 2026)
Selected for NIH-funded Summer Institute in Biostatistics and Data Science. Conducted mentored research in statistical modeling with reproducible workflows for biomedical data analysis.


Industry & Applied
Data Analyst · Invest Atlanta (Feb 2026 – May 2026)
Geospatial and demographic analysis evaluating fresh food access across Atlanta neighborhoods. Developed gravity-weighted demand model to identify underserved areas and priority grant recipients.
Medical Research Intern · Berkeley Pharma Tech, Remote (Aug 2025 – Jan 2026)
Led a 4-member cross-university team on neuromodulation treatments for treatment-resistant depression. Authored core manuscript sections and directed submission per AMA citation standards.
AI Trainer · Handshake AI, Remote (Jul – Aug 2025)
Designed and stress-tested domain-specific prompts to benchmark LLM performance in biomedical research tasks. Assessed model outputs for scientific accuracy and technical depth.

section: Anchors Against Food Deserts: Gravity Model of Atlanta’s Grocery Network
text:
Anchors Against Food Deserts: Gravity Model of Atlanta’s Grocery Network
Invest Atlanta · Emory Center for AI Learning · 2025
With Lorayne Gulbrandsen, Jessica (Jiayi) Wang, Alice Xu
Identified “hero stores” — grocery retailers most critical to preventing food deserts — using a gravity-weighted demand model across Atlanta tax parcels. Built for Invest Atlanta to support grant prioritization decisions.
Methods: Gravity model with inverse-square distance decay, Gaussian kernel density estimation, capture rate normalization. Top hero store: Eat Right Atlanta – Emory Midtown (contribution score: 9,517).
Tools: Python · GeoPandas · Folium · contextily · NumPy · pandas · matplotlib



Invest Atlanta Poster


Download Poster

Recent Highlights

📄 Published — Heritability of long-term complications in classic galactosemia. Journal of Inherited Metabolic Disease, 2026. doi:10.1002/jimd.70181
🏥 Winship Cancer Institute — Paper in progress on PDAC immunotherapy trial data
🎓 NIH SIBDS — Selected for Summer Institute in Biostatistics & Data Science at Columbia Mailman (Summer 2026)
🏆 ASA DataFest @ Emory — Judges’ Choice Award (2026)
📊 SOA Exam P — Passed · FM in preparation

Areas of Work


🧬 Biostatistics & Bioinformatics
Statistical analysis of biological and clinical data. Projects span survival analysis, genomic data workflows, and epidemiological modeling — with an eye toward PhD-level research in computational biology or biostatistics.


📐 Mathematical & Statistical Modeling
Rigorous quantitative modeling applied to real-world problems. Competed in 7 math modeling competitions (3× HiMCM, 2× IMMC, 2× MTFC), co-founded Newton North’s Math Modeling Club, and presented at the 2023 Joint Mathematics Meeting in Boston, MA.

HiMCM — Meritorious (2021) & Finalist (2023), issued by COMAP
IMMC — USA National Finalist (2022 & 2024), issued by COMAP

Competition papers: HiMCM 2021 · HiMCM 2022 · HiMCM 2023 · IMMC 2022 · IMMC 2024


💼 Business & Quantitative Case Studies
Quantitative approaches to structured problems — risk modeling, data-driven strategy, and applied statistics. Adaptable to consulting, actuarial, and policy contexts.
Modeling the Future Challenge (Actuarial Foundation) — NE Regional #1 (2023), National Finalist (2023 & 2024)
Actuarial modeling papers: MTFC 2023 · MTFC 2024
Anchors Against Food Deserts — gravity-weighted demand surface model identifying “hero stores” critical to Atlanta’s fresh food network, built for Invest Atlanta using GeoPandas, Folium, and kernel density estimation. View Poster
Earlier work: Housing Fluctuation & the Case-Shiller Index — analyzing single-family home price trends across Boston, NYC, and San Francisco (2001–2021) using housing supply, median income, and population as explanatory factors. Built in R.


🛠 Tools & Reproducible Research
R, Python, Quarto, and Shiny for end-to-end reproducible analysis. I care about clean code, clear documentation, and results others can verify and build on.
Bro’s Statistical Analyzer — an R Shiny app with a full UI for data ingestion, transformation (pivot, aggregate, reshape), 11 statistical tests (normality, t-tests, ANOVA, Wilcoxon, chi-square, Fisher’s exact), and 6 ggplot2 visualization types with reproducible code output.



Currently open to research opportunities, internships, and collaborations.
